batchLabel: Backend Shared Module — Utilities, Errors, Middleware, Sync Services, Types, Events

files.summary: AES-256-GCM symmetric encryption/decryption for field-level data. Uses node:crypto with a hex key from config. Provides `encrypt`, `decrypt`, and `encryptMany` (batch field encryption). Returns base64-encoded ciphertext, IV, and auth tag.
keyPoints: Static algorithm (aes-256-gcm) with 12-byte IV
Key derived from config.ENCRYPTION_KEY as hex buffer
encryptMany loops Object.entries for bulk field encryption

files.summary: Password hashing via argon2id (memory 64MB, time 3, parallelism 4), plus SHA-256 salted OTP hashing and generic SHA-256 token hashing.
keyPoints: argon2id with OWASP-recommended parameters
OTP hash uses random 16-char hex salt + SHA-256(salt+otp)
hashToken is plain SHA-256 hex digest

files.summary: Generates cryptographically random numeric OTP (default 6 digits) using node:crypto randomInt. Checks expiration against a Date.
keyPoints: Uses crypto.randomInt (not Math.random) for security
isOtpExpiresAt compares current time to expiresAt Date

files.summary: Offset-based pagination with cursor encoding. Parses page/limit from query params (clamped to config max), builds meta with total, page, limit, and a base64 cursor for hasMore.
keyPoints: Cursor is base64-encoded 'nextPage:limit' string
page defaults to 1, limit clamped to PAGINATION_DEFAULTS.maxLimit
PaginationMeta returned inside PaginatedResult wrapper

files.summary: Converts HTML to PDF using Playwright (headless Chromium). Singleton browser instance, A4 format with 20/15px margins, prints background graphics.
keyPoints: Lazy singleton browser initialization
New context per call — closes after PDF generation
Returns Buffer

files.summary: Standardized Fastify reply helpers using ApiResponse<T> shape. Methods for success (200), paginated (200), created (201), and no-content (204).
keyPoints: Consistent { success: true, data } envelope via satisfies
sendCreated delegates to sendSuccess with status 201
Typed with ApiResponse<T> generic

files.summary: Generic circuit-breaker with CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN states. Configurable failure threshold (default 3) and reset timeout (default 30s). Supports optional fallback function.
keyPoints: 3 states: CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN
Auto-transitions to HALF_OPEN after resetTimeout
Fallback invoked on failure if provided, else error thrown
Console.warn on circuit open

files.summary: Base application error with statusCode, machine-readable code, and optional field. All custom errors inherit from this.
keyPoints: Extends Error with name='AppError'
Properties: message, statusCode, code, field

files.summary: Concrete error extending AppError with hardcoded 400 status and VALIDATION_ERROR code. Optional field for pointing to the invalid input.
keyPoints: Wrapper: AppError(message, 400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR', field)

files.summary: Fastify preHandler that verifies access token (Bearer header or cookie). Checks Redis blacklist, refreshes dealer online presence, and attaches user payload to request.
keyPoints: Supports Bearer header and access_token cookie
Catches TokenExpiredError specifically
Redis blacklist check + online presence heartbeat (15min TTL)
Degraded mode if Redis unavailable (swallows error)
Sets request.user with { id, role, fps_id }

files.summary: Role-based access control middleware. `authorize(...roles)` checks user.role is in allowed list. `authorizeSelf()` allows admin or same-ID access only.
keyPoints: authorize is a higher-order function returning middleware
authorizeSelf checks id param vs request.user.id

files.summary: Inserts audit trail rows into audit_logs table with before/after data snapshots, IP, and user agent. Silently fails on DB error.
keyPoints: Stores old_data/new_data as JSON stringified
Captures request.ip and user-agent header
Console.error on failure (non-blocking)

files.summary: POST-only idempotency key middleware. Validates key format (hex UUID-like, 8-128 chars), caches response in Redis for 24h, and replays cached response on repeat key.
keyPoints: Skips non-POST methods
Initial cache stores { statusCode: 200, body: null } as placeholder (not final response)
24-hour TTL

files.summary: Cleans and standardizes raw sync records for PDS/ration data. Normalizes names (title case), mobile numbers (+91 prefix), dates (multiple formats → ISO date), addresses, ration card numbers, and numeric fields.
keyPoints: toTitleCase strips non-alpha chars, lowercases then capitalizes first letter per word
normalizeMobile strips non-digits, adds +91 prefix for 10-digit Indian numbers
normalizeDate handles YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
normalizeNumeric strips non-numeric except '.'
Returns shallow copy with normalized fields

files.summary: Rule-based validator for four PDS record types (beneficiary, transaction, stock_allocation, lifting_record). Rules: required fields, numeric range, date format, ration card format, and lifted-vs-allocated quantity check.
keyPoints: 4 record types with different required field sets
LIFTED_EXCEEDS_ALLOCATED is a warning (not error)
validateBatch splits into pass/fail arrays
Ration card rule: 4-20 chars, alphanumeric + / - only

files.summary: Inserts failed validation records into sync_quarantine table. Provides summary grouped by record_type and error code, optionally filtered by dealer.
keyPoints: Accepts batch of QuarantineEntry objects
Records stored with raw_data and errors as jsonb
Source defaults to 'playwright'
Summary uses JSONB_AGG for aggregate counts by type and error code

files.summary: Insert and query system events with severity levels (info/warn/error/critical), source, optional trace ID, and metadata. Insert is best-effort (silent catch).
keyPoints: 4 severity levels with critical for highest
getRecentEvents optionally filters by severity
Insert wraps in try/catch — non-blocking

files.summary: TypeScript interfaces for all core PDS entities: Dealer, Session, OtpRequest, Beneficiary, StockAllocation, Transaction, AuditLog, Notification, plus shared API shapes (PaginationMeta, ApiResponse, ApiError, JwtPayload, AuthUser).
keyPoints: All entity types use string IDs (UUIDs)
Dates are ISO 8601 strings
ApiResponse uses optional meta for paginated responses
AuthUser mirrors JwtPayload for request.user shape

files.summary: Const object enums with derived TypeScript types. Covers Role (dealer/area_officer/admin), AccountStatus, CommodityType (6 commodities), BeneficiaryCategory (APL/BPL/AAY/PHH), TransactionMode (pos/manual/otg), NotificationType, and OtpPurpose.
keyPoints: All use 'as const' pattern for literal union types
Commodities: Rice, Wheat, Sugar, Kerosene, Oil, Pulses
Transaction modes include OTG (offline transaction gadget)
Otp purposes: password_reset, verify_mobile, 2fa

files.summary: Simple event bus that asynchronously enqueues domain events into a job queue. Defines event type constants for key domain actions (transactions, lifting, commissions, stock, beneficiaries, allocations, sync, dashboard refresh, govt sync).
keyPoints: emit() lazily imports domain-events.queue and enqueues
Error is caught and logged (non-blocking)
Singleton eventBus instance exported
removeAll() is intentionally no-op; handlers on worker side

keyFindings: Utilities cover encryption (AES-256-GCM), hashing (argon2id), OTP generation (crypto.randomInt), pagination (offset + base64 cursor), PDF generation (Playwright), standardized HTTP responses, and circuit-breaker resilience.
Error hierarchy: AppError base class → ValidationError (400). AuthError, ForbiddenError, SessionNotFoundError referenced but defined elsewhere.
Authentication uses JWT + Redis for blacklist/online tracking with degraded-mode fallback. Authorization supports role-based and self-only access.
Sync pipeline: normalization.service (cleanse raw import) → validation.service (rule-based pass/fail) → quarantine.service (store failures). System events provide observability.
Models cover 7 domain entities (Dealer, Session, OtpRequest, Beneficiary, StockAllocation, Transaction, AuditLog, Notification) plus shared API shapes.
Enums use TypeScript 'as const' pattern for 7 domain enumerations with literal union types.
Event bus is lightweight — delegates to a job queue for async processing. No in-process handler registry.
